commit.message:
Seed January 2026 data — 51 transactions

$3,755 income, $2,026 expenses. Education mapped to other category.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: modern-retro design overhaul (JetBrains Mono + wireframe style)

- Switch font to JetBrains Mono throughout
- Warm off-white background (#f5f0e8), almost-black text, deep red accent (#bf1a0a)
- Zero border-radius everywhere — sharp, geometric elements
- Wireframe buttons (.btn-wire) that invert to deep red on hover
- Bottom-border-only inputs (.input-line)
- Redesign HeroSection, StatusDashboard, AIAssistant, GlobalDiary to match
- High-contrast sidebar: inverted active nav item, thick left red border

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove dead editor config and openConfig macro type

The editor field (ALLOWED_EDITORS, EditorName type, normalizeEditor,
DEFAULT_EDITOR, MAX_EDITOR_LENGTH) and openConfig macro type were
never wired to a working handler — openConfig was accepted by the
type system and rendered as a button, but pressing it sent an action
the bridge silently ignored. Remove all traces from config, app,
bridge-client, layouts, PI, and tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correctly detect white/Caucasian avatar skin tone for family B-roll scenes

buildFamilyResemblanceNote now recognizes 'white woman', 'caucasian',
etc. as fair-skin indicators so family members in B-roll scenes get
the correct complexion. Previously only 'fair skin'/'pale skin' were
detected, causing white avatars to produce no family cue and the AI
defaulting to brown skin for adult children.

Also strengthened AVATAR LIKENESS RULES in system prompt with explicit
race extraction examples and a hard warning against defaulting to brown
skin when the avatar is white/Caucasian.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete missing features + visual overhaul

- Add Skills & Academia tab (was fully built but hidden)
- AI chat: persistent conversation history saved to Supabase
- AI chat: full user data context in system prompt (all keys + formats)
- AI chat: increase step limit to 10, remove console logs
- GMAT exam date now editable (was hardcoded)
- Countdown title now editable (was hardcoded 'Bocconi')
- HeroSection: full Claude-style redesign (dark card, clean grid, orange accent on days)
- StatusDashboard: Claude-style redesign using CSS vars, clean stat cards
- AIAssistant: Claude-style UI, persistent history, clear button

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: convert all marketing pages to Server Components for SSR/crawlability

All marketing pages (about, product, security, pricing, developers, alpha)
now render as pure Server Components with static metadata exports.
Removes 'use client', framer-motion, useState animations that prevented
crawlers from seeing page content.

- About: pure Server Component (already done in prior commit, cleaned up)
- Product: pure Server Component with pillars, flow, tools, infra badges
- Security: removed useState animation (opacity:0 start = invisible to crawlers)
- Pricing: static annual pricing, competitor table, FAQ (no billing toggle)
- Developers: static code examples (Node.js, Python, cURL), API endpoint list
- Alpha: Server Component with small Client island for signup form only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release version 1.0.2

Bug fixes and improvements:
- Fixed Excel column mapping from 1-based @DataColumn order to 0-based indices
- Added BigDecimal support in FormatProvider for proper type parsing
- Fixed LocalDate parsing for Excel serial dates
- Added workaround for Excel 1900 leap year bug
- Improved empty dataset handling in ExcelDatasetWriter
- Enhanced test coverage with LocalDateTest

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

fix: Repair test suite — 75 of 137 files now passing

- Fix PHP 8.5 deprecation: PDO::MYSQL_ATTR_SSL_CA → Pdo\Mysql compat
- Replace Log::fake() with Log::spy() in 3 test files
- Fix 2FA redirect in admin test routes (withoutMiddleware)
- Add missing RefreshDatabase trait to 4 test files
- Add missing admin.files.retry-failed route
- Remove dead isTransientError() tests from UploadToGoogleDriveJobTest
- Delete QueueTestServiceComprehensiveTest (all methods removed)
- Delete SetupSecurityServiceTest (all methods removed)
- Update test-suite-errors.md with current status

commit.message:
Add LLM SEO: llms.txt, robots.txt, sitemap, JSON-LD, OpenGraph metadata

- Add llms.txt for AI crawler discoverability (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity)
- Add robots.ts allowing AI bots, blocking /cabinet and /auth
- Add sitemap.ts with all public routes and priorities
- Add JSON-LD structured data (MedicalOrganization + WebSite schema)
- Expand root metadata with OpenGraph, Twitter Cards, canonical URL
- Add metadata layouts for /likari, /kliniky, /onlayn-konsultaciyi

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Oracle Hub, conversational /ask, affinity system, economy, trade picker fix

- Add /oracle command with 5 intelligence modes: TSL League, Open Intel,
  Sports Intel, Player Scout, and Strategy Room
- Improve /ask quality with few-shot SQL examples, common pitfall warnings,
  and structured answer formatting guidelines
- Add per-user conversation memory (5-turn, 30-min TTL) so follow-up
  questions retain context across both /ask and Oracle Hub
- Add user affinity system (affinity.py) — ATLAS adapts tone based on how
  users treat it, with keyword sentiment analysis and 4 affinity tiers
- Fix /trades team select hitting Discord's 25-item limit by adding
  AFC/NFC conference selector flow (16 teams per conference)
- Add economy system (economy_cog.py) with TSL Bucks, stipends, and
  /commish eco admin commands
- Add casino ledger renderer (Pillow-based) and per-game channel support
- Fix critical import bug: oracle_cog was importing from old history_cog
  instead of codex_cog, causing outdated prompts and missing features
- Wire affinity + conversation memory into Oracle Hub TSL modal
- Add /oracle hint to standalone /ask footer

commit.message:
Fix haptic feedback on Android using Vibration API fallback

expo-haptics impactAsync doesn't work well on Android/Expo Go.
Use Vibration.vibrate() as fallback on Android (10ms for light,
20ms for medium feedback).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: edit campaign brief inline from overview page

Add pencil Edit button to Campaign Brief section. Clicking it transforms
the static display into inline editable fields for all brief properties:
title, persona, archetype, tone, duration, phone, phonetic, offer,
deadline, benefit, affordability, CTA style, and notes. Save persists
via updateCampaign repo + updateCampaignAction, Cancel restores values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add profile edit mode, updateProfile API, and expand nationalities

- ProfileView now has edit button (pencil icon) in header that toggles
  inline editing of name, gender, age range, and nationality
- Added updateProfile() to AuthContext that updates fields without
  overwriting createdAt (unlike saveProfile which is for initial setup)
- Expanded NATIONALITIES from 30 to 150+ covering every world region,
  grouped by geography with Gulf/Middle East first for Abu Dhabi users
- Nationality picker has full-screen search for easy filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: allow Vercel origin in CORS config

Reads APP_BASE_URL and optional ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var to dynamically
add production frontend URLs to the CORS allowlist. Previously only
localhost origins were permitted, blocking all deployed frontend requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Standardise all gaps on Gaffer page to 24px (space-y-6)

Removed pt-4 from teams container and pt-6 from Share Teams wrapper so space-y-6 handles all spacing uniformly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade GitHub Actions to Node.js 24 compatible versions

- actions/checkout v4 -> v6
- actions/setup-node v4 -> v6
- stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action v5 -> v7

Node.js 20 actions are deprecated and will be forced to Node.js 24
starting June 2nd, 2026.

repository.name: mhr-website
repository.description: Mile High Runners Website

commit.message:
Post-GPT sanitization, merge audit logging, 5-page PDF safety

- Add _sanitize_extracted_data() layer after GPT analysis: type coercion
  for numeric fields (handles German comma format, currency suffixes),
  enum validation (object_type, usage, employment_type), date format
  normalization (DD.MM.YYYY → ISO), plausibility warnings
- Fix German thousands separator parsing ("3.500" → 3500, not 3.5)
- Log all fact overwrites in merge_facts() with old/new values,
  stored in audit_log for broker traceability
- Strip months_covered from truncated scanned PDFs (>5 pages rendered)
  so document counts as 1 and case stays incomplete
- Revert Google Drive link labels to simple "Google Drive (1/2)"
- Simplify GDrive import to single button

The AI was generating soft lifestyle b-roll (families at dinner, children
playing, sunlit backyards) instead of scroll-stopping pattern-interrupt
visuals. Added explicit FORBIDDEN list and required type mandates:
document reveals, crowd/social tension, raw face micro-emotions.

repository.description: Upload Drive-In, a self-hosted file upload service that integrates with Google Drive. It's built with Laravel using the Kiro IDE.

commit.message:
Add Getty fetcher, fix report bias, fix calendar UI bugs

- Getty fetcher: scrapes getty.edu/visit/cal for Center + Villa events,
  handles recurring programs and specific-date events
- Report diversity: institutional/nightlife bucketing (60/40 split),
  per-source caps, and prompt updated to require mixed coverage
- Outlook API: priority_events now includes id, start_at, source_url,
  description for correct modal rendering
- Fix outlook pill clicks showing wrong event (index mismatch between
  priority_events and _events arrays, now matched by id)
- Fix calendar day-click modal rendering as unstyled HTML at page bottom
  (was using nonexistent modal-overlay class, now uses event-modal-overlay)
- Report "last updated" widget with relative timestamps
- Tide curve smoothing: replace flat-tangent beziers with Catmull-Rom
  spline to eliminate micro-wobbles
- Removed hardcoded health endpoint defaults in favor of required env vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(test): harden timing-sensitive tests against parallel-suite load

Two integration/e2e tests were flaky when run alongside the Playwright
browser suite due to system resource contention:

cli-beads-workspace.integration.test.ts — --at filter test:
  The test inserted bead snapshots with `strftime('now', '-3 hours')` and
  `-1 hour`, then queried with `--at 2h` (a relative duration).  Under
  heavy parallel load the subprocess spawned by fly() evaluates Date.now()
  several hundred ms later than the SQLite insertion, causing the cutoff
  calculation to drift.  Fixed by using fixed absolute ISO 8601 timestamps
  (2025-06-01T09:00:00Z and 11:00:00Z) and querying with an explicit cutoff
  (2025-06-01T10:00:00Z) — entirely deterministic regardless of wall-clock
  timing or system load.

cli-config-and-state.integration.test.ts — providers separate from ssh:
  The test called `fly(["doctor"], env())` which spawns a subprocess that
  runs the SSH connectivity check against 127.0.0.1:22.  In CI the runner's
  sshd is listening, so node-ssh establishes the TCP connection before
  rejecting the invalid key.  This takes 1-10s; combined with the 15s
  spawnSync timeout there was no margin for the subprocess to flush stdout
  before being killed.  Fixed by replacing the subprocess call with direct
  TypeScript API calls to loadSshConfig() and loadProvidersConfig(), which
  test the config-loading logic without touching SSH connectivity.

cli-concurrent test:
  Added { timeout: 25_000 } to the 5-subprocess read test so it doesn't
  hit the global 10s ceiling when Playwright tests hold CPU.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reranker 500 error — add -ub/-b 4096 batch size flags

Root cause: llama-server default physical batch size (-ub 512) too small
for token-dense chunks. Single query-document pairs exceeding 512 tokens
caused HTTP 500. Fix adds -ub 4096 -b 4096 to reranker server flags and
restores RERANK_CANDIDATES from 10 back to 50.

Includes debug suite in dev/llama_server/ with numbered scripts pattern:
- 01_capture_stderr: start reranker with log capture
- 02_reproduce_500: stress test with --db/--synthetic/--verify/--compare
- 03_test_batch_size: test different -b values

commit.message:
Fix canvas drag-and-drop: detect swaps via per-instance settings

The StreamDeck SDK doesn't change action positions on canvas drag —
it swaps the per-instance settings instead. Store macroIndex in each
action's settings so mismatches between stored and physical position
reveal drag-swaps. Also auto-apply PI drag reorder on drop.

commit.message:
Fix kiwix viewer hash format — use book name, not full content path

The kiwix viewer prepends /content/ to the hash internally via
userUrl2IframeUrl(). Using #/content/{zim} caused a doubled path.
The correct format is #{zim}/ (just the book name with trailing slash).

commit.message:
fix: expand quote box to single line, no wrapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web-ui): remove orphaned CSI stripping from safeTruncateAnsi

safeTruncateAnsi was unconditionally stripping trailing bracket+digit
patterns (\[\??[0-9;]*$) on the assumption they were partial orphaned
CSI sequences. This corrupted plain non-ANSI text when the 1000-char
cutoff landed on literal content like "[12" (array indices, log output).

Fix: Remove the orphaned CSI regex from safeTruncateAnsi entirely.
The function now only strips real ESC byte sequences (\x1b) split by
truncation. Orphaned CSI cleanup is already handled downstream by
ansiToHtml/stripOrphanedCsi during rendering.

Added 3 regression tests for plain text preservation at boundaries.

Task: @task-ansi-terminal-output-rendering
Spec: @ansi-terminal-rendering
